text.title: Solo Incident Triage Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Solo Incident Triage Checklist methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every incident has a named operator owning it from detection to closure. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership during an incident = action paralysis.

rule.statement: First customer-facing comms (status page or email) MUST go out within 15 minutes of detection. Even 'we are investigating' counts.
rule.rationale: Silence amplifies anger more than the bug. Acknowledgement buys patience.

rule.statement: CONTAIN (rollback / feature-flag off) MUST be attempted BEFORE forward-fix on prod. Forward-fix without containment is forbidden unless containment is impossible.
rule.rationale: Forward-fix on a flaming prod is how 1-hour incidents become 6-hour incidents.

rule.statement: Postmortem MUST happen within 72h of resolution, MUST list root cause + ≥1 corrective action, MUST avoid naming individuals (system flaws, not human flaws).
rule.rationale: Blameful postmortems suppress reporting; suppressed incidents recur.

rule.statement: Any forward-fix deployed during an incident MUST be tested in a local repro OR previously-prepared staging clone — NEVER 'I think this will work'.
rule.rationale: Untested forward-fixes during incidents are the textbook cause of compound outages.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-solo-incident-triage-checklist.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
